sections.paragraph: [Hauran], pr.n. of a region beyond Jordan, situated eastward of Gaulanitis (גֹּולָן) and Batanæa, and to the west of Trachonitis (now el Lejah), extending from Jabbok to the territory of Damascus, Eze 47:16, 18 Eze 47:18Gr. Αὐρανῖτις, Ὠρανῖτις; Arabic حَوْرَانُ. It undoubtedly takes its name from the number of its caverns (חֹור), in which even now the inhabitants of the region dwell. See a more full account of this district in Burckhardt’s Travels in Syria and Palestine, page 111, seqq.; 393, seqq.; 446; Germ. ed.
